brands.name: Pampered Peach Wax Bar, The
brands.notes: Tampa-based luxury waxing-salon franchise founded by aesthetician Jessica Kustron; franchising since 2022 with a low-cost 'Suite To Salon' entry model and a membership (VI-Peach Club) recurring-revenue component. Unit counts reported by the franchisor (70+ locations) far exceed directory FDD-derived counts (~14), so verify the current FDD Item 20 before relying on system size.

riskFlags.title: Large unit-count discrepancy between franchisor marketing and directory data
riskFlags.detail: The franchisor's site claims '70+ locations' while Entrepreneur's FDD-derived directory lists 14 total units (2026 edition). The marketing figure may count sold/suite-stage territories rather than open salons.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise Directory vs. franchisor site

riskFlags.title: Very new, small franchise system
riskFlags.detail: Founded 2019, franchising only since 2022; even by the franchisor's own claims the system is young, and FDD-derived counts show ~14 units. Limited multi-year franchisee track record.

riskFlags.title: AUV figure is a marketing claim, not verified Item 19 data
riskFlags.detail: The franchisor advertises an average unit volume of $359K-$1M on its franchise page; no publicly verifiable Item 19 disclosure was found to substantiate it, so it is excluded from revenue fields here.

riskFlags.title: Conflicting franchise-fee figures across sources
riskFlags.detail: America's Best Franchises and SharpSheets list a $15,000 franchise fee, while Entrepreneur shows no/zero initial fee and Franchise Gator has shown $45,000 with a different (likely stale) investment range. Confirm against the current FDD.
riskFlags.source: America's Best Franchises vs. Entrepreneur vs. Franchise Gator

nationwide.note: Broker listing states franchises are available throughout the United States; franchisor's page markets nationwide expansion with a regulated-states legal disclaimer but publishes no per-state open/closed list.

franchiseFee.note: Corroborated by SharpSheets FDD summary; Entrepreneur shows no initial fee and Franchise Gator shows $45,000 — verify against current FDD

royaltyPercent.note: 6% of gross sales, plus ~$595-$735/month technology fee per directory listings

liquidCapitalMin.note: Entrepreneur lists $100K liquid cash; America's Best Franchises lists $50K minimum cash

totalUnits.note: FDD-derived directory count; franchisor markets '70+ locations' (likely includes sold territories/suites)

veteranDiscount.note: Entrepreneur lists veterans receive 5% off training fees

isGrowing.note: Entrepreneur shows +180% unit growth from a very small base

fddAvailable.note: Franchisor references FDD review and Discovery Day in its process

brands.name: Pink Zebra Moving
brands.notes: Birmingham, AL local-moving franchise founded in 2020 by Ron Holt (founder/former CEO of Two Maids), franchising since April 2022 with a customer-experience-led positioning. Fast territory sales but a young open-unit base; reported unit counts vary widely by source and the initial franchise fee rose materially in the 2026 FDD.

riskFlags.title: Very new system with widely divergent unit counts
riskFlags.detail: Founded 2020, franchising since 2022. Reported counts range from 8 (2025 FDD per FDD Exchange) and 14 (Vetted Biz, 2025 FDD) to 19 (Entrepreneur 2026) and 62 'open units' claimed by broker site Franchise Sidekick — suggesting many sold-but-not-yet-open territories. Verify current Item 20.

riskFlags.title: Franchise fee increased sharply in 2026 FDD
riskFlags.detail: The franchisor's own investment page (citing the 2026 FDD) lists initial franchise fees of $52,500-$75,000, versus the $30,000 fee shown in earlier FDD-derived directory data.
riskFlags.source: Pink Zebra Moving Franchising — Expected Investment

riskFlags.title: No lawsuits or bankruptcy disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz notes the FDD does not disclose litigation or bankruptcy; standard operating risks for moving businesses (labor management, local competition) apply.

franchiseFee.note: Franchisor's 2026 FDD-cited range is $52,500-$75,000 for a standard territory; earlier 2025 FDD data showed $30,000

totalInvestmentMin.note: Franchisor-stated 2026 range $128,368-$260,679 incl. first 90 days; 2025 FDD range was $131,032-$249,398

royaltyPercent.note: 7% on booked revenue, reported as scaling down to 5% at higher volume; Vetted Biz lists 6% — confirm tier structure in current FDD

liquidCapitalMin.note: Net worth requirement $175,000 per same source

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19-derived average yearly gross sales per location, 2025 FDD (Vetted Biz); broker site Franchise Sidekick cites $1,072,327 average booked revenues — small, young unit base, treat with caution

totalUnits.note: Counts vary: 14 per 2025 FDD (Vetted Biz), 19 per Entrepreneur 2026, 62 open / 82 awarded per Franchise Sidekick broker listing

yearFounded.note: Pink Zebra Moving, LLC formed August 14, 2020 (Alabama) per FDD

yearFranchising.note: Began offering franchises April 15, 2022 per FDD

multiUnitAvailable.note: Franchisor offers multiple agreements for adjoining territories

veteranDiscount.note: Broker listing states no veteran discount; Entrepreneur lists veteran incentive as N/A

isGrowing.note: 30 territories awarded in last 12 months per broker listing; Entrepreneur shows +111% unit growth

item19Available.note: Item 19 includes data from locations open at least one year

brands.name: Pvolve
brands.notes: Boutique functional-fitness studio brand founded 2017 (NYC), franchising since 2020 and in an aggressive expansion phase: ~25-30 studios open across the U.S. and Canada with 80+ licenses awarded by early 2026. Capital-intensive build-out with a young franchised-unit track record; no formal AUV disclosure found in the FDD.

riskFlags.title: Young franchised system; large gap between licenses awarded and studios open
riskFlags.detail: Only 13 franchised locations per 2025 FDD data while the franchisor announced 80+ licenses awarded nationwide by January 2026 and 50+ studios 'in development' — most of the system is unopened and unproven.
riskFlags.source: Franzy (2025 FDD data) / PRNewswire franchise announcements

riskFlags.title: High, wide investment band for boutique fitness
riskFlags.detail: Total investment reported at $454K-$800K (Entrepreneur) to as wide as $393K-$893K (Franchise Payback), driven by leasehold improvements of $128K-$490K — significant capital at risk in a competitive category.

riskFlags.title: No formal AUV disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: Franchise Payback notes the FDD does not formally disclose average unit revenue (third-party estimate ~$882K is unverified), so revenue expectations rest on limited data.

nationwide.note: Listed as offering franchises throughout the United States; 2025-26 announcements include new agreements/leases in NV, CA, AZ, TX, KY, NY and Canada.

franchiseFee.note: Single-unit fee; Entrepreneur (2025 FDD data) lists $52,500; discounted fees for multi-unit developers

totalInvestmentMin.note: Entrepreneur range $454,250-$799,600; Franchise Payback cites a wider $393K-$893K

adFundPercent.note: Plus a fixed monthly marketing/brand spend reported by Franchise Payback (~$3,000/month)

liquidCapitalMin.note: Franchisor's current site: $200K liquid / $500K net worth; Entrepreneur lists $250K liquid / $750K net worth from earlier FDD data

totalUnits.note: Athletech News reported ~30 studios open across U.S./Canada by mid-2025; 80+ licenses awarded per franchisor PR (Jan 2026)

yearFranchising.note: Some trackers (Franchise Payback) date active franchising to 2021; first franchised studios opened later

exclusiveTerritories.note: Franchise Payback describes protected territories of ~50,000 population including digital-membership exclusivity

veteranDiscount.note: 20% off first-unit franchise fee for veterans

multiUnitAvailable.note: Discounted franchise fee for multi-unit developers

isGrowing.note: 50+ studios in development per IFA-published announcement; multiple new leases/agreements through 2025-26

fddAvailable.note: 2025 FDD on file with franchise trackers

brands.name: Renovation Sells
brands.notes: Chicago-based, home-based presale home-renovation franchise (design + light remodel to prep listings for sale), founded by Michael Valente and franchising since 2020 with roughly 40 units. Low entry cost for the category; owner-operator model with no absentee or part-time operation and no exclusive territories per directory data.

riskFlags.title: Young, fast-scaling system
riskFlags.detail: Franchising only since 2020 and scaled to ~39-43 units within a few years (Entrepreneur shows +3,800% growth from a tiny base); long-term unit economics and resale market are unproven, and performance is tied to housing-market transaction volume.

riskFlags.title: No exclusive territories
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur's FDD-derived listing shows exclusive territories are NOT available, unusual for a home-services brand — verify territory terms in the current FDD.

riskFlags.title: No public Item 19 figures verified
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz indicates yearly gross sales data exists behind its paywall (2024 FDD) but no verifiable Item 19 average revenue figure is publicly available; system-aggregate numbers circulating on lead-gen sites are unreliable and were excluded.

royaltyPercent.note: Entrepreneur and Vetted Biz list 7%; SharpSheets has cited 8% — confirm in current FDD

liquidCapitalMin.note: Net worth requirement $350,000 per same source; Vetted Biz shows $20K minimum cash

totalUnits.note: Other trackers report 41 (Vetted Biz) to 43 (SharpSheets) franchised locations

yearFounded.note: Vetted Biz lists 2020; company founded in Chicago by Michael Valente

yearFranchising.note: 1851 Franchise article describes offering franchises 'since 2021'; Entrepreneur and Vetted Biz both show 2020

isGrowing.note: Rapid unit growth since 2020 from a small base

fddAvailable.note: 2024 FDD on file with franchise trackers

brands.name: Roof Resource, The
brands.notes: Michigan-founded (2020) home-based residential roofing concept that franchised in 2023, but has since rebranded as 'SkyeVantage powered by The Roof Resource' and pivoted from franchising to a lower-cost licensing model (October 2025), expanding into windows, gutters and siding across a ~13-state footprint. Legacy franchise-economics data below reflects the 2023-25 franchise offering and is largely superseded by the license program.

riskFlags.title: Brand rebranded to SkyeVantage and pivoted from franchising to licensing
riskFlags.detail: The company now operates as 'SkyeVantage powered by The Roof Resource' (site notes a move to SkyeVantage.com) and, per IFPG, switched to a licensing model in October 2025 ($14,500 per-territory license, ~$26,440 total investment, $995/month flat fee). The traditional 'The Roof Resource' franchise offering documented in 2023-25 FDD data appears no longer to be what is sold.

riskFlags.title: Very small, very new system
riskFlags.detail: Founded 2020, first franchise sold 2023; only ~9-12 units (7 franchised + 2 company-owned per 2025 FDD data, 'fewer than 20' per IFPG in 2026). No Item 19 financial performance representation is made.

riskFlags.title: Broad franchisor reserved rights; territory protection limited under franchise model
riskFlags.detail: Franchise Payback notes the franchisor retained rights to operate similar businesses and sell via alternative channels even within a franchisee's ~175,000-household marketing territory, making protection effectively non-exclusive under the legacy franchise agreement. (The 2026 SkyeVantage license program markets 'exclusive territory rights.')

riskFlags.title: Conflicting fee/investment figures across sources
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur (FDD-derived) lists a $45,000 fee, 10% royalty, 1% ad fee and $89K-$116K investment; Vetted Biz/Franchise Payback pages have shown a $75,000 fee, 7% royalty and other ranges; IFPG's 2026 license listing shows $14,500. Figures depend heavily on which offering (franchise vs. license) and which year.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur vs. Vetted Biz vs. IFPG

nationwide.source: IFPG listing (SkyeVantage/The Roof Resource license program)
nationwide.note: License program marketed as 'available in all 50 states'; actual operating footprint is ~13 states (per ClickOnDetroit, Mar 2026) with locations listed in MI, OH, IN, TX, FL and others.

franchiseFee.note: Legacy franchise offering (2025 FDD-derived). Successor SkyeVantage license program is $14,500 per territory (IFPG, 2026)

totalInvestmentMin.note: Legacy franchise range $89,067-$115,683 (Entrepreneur); Franchise Payback cited $120K-$146K; 2026 license model ~$26,440 all-in per IFPG

royaltyPercent.note: Legacy franchise: 10% of gross sales plus 1% marketing; 2026 license model instead charges a $995/month flat fee per IFPG

liquidCapitalMin.note: Franchisor franchise site: $150K liquidity / $350K net worth / 700 credit score for the franchise offering; IFPG 2026 license listing shows only $25K-$50K cash required

totalUnits.note: 7 franchised + 2 company-owned (2025 FDD data); Entrepreneur lists 12; IFPG (2026) says fewer than 20 open

yearFounded.note: Founded in Macomb Township, MI; HQ Sterling Heights, MI

employeesRequired.note: Legacy franchise listing; license model leaves staffing to the licensee

canRunFromHome.note: Marketed as the first home-based roofing franchise; no office, warehouse, crews or trucks

absenteeOwnership.note: Semi-absentee/passive ownership permitted per IFPG; Entrepreneur also lists absentee ownership as allowed

multiUnitAvailable.note: Master/multi-territory opportunities offered under the license program

item19Available.note: FDD does not disclose average unit revenue; IFPG confirms no Item 19 offered

fddAvailable.note: FDD existed for the 2023-25 franchise offering; the 2026 SkyeVantage license program may not be FDD-governed
